「?たら」 is a conditional 'if/when' that also expresses sequence. (practice variation 20)
「〜終わる」 attaches to verb stems to mean 'finish doing' something.
「〜てしまう」 can express completion or regret ('end up doing').
「〜に限って」 means 'only / exclusively'.
「〜に向けて」 means 'toward / in preparation for'.
「〜ば〜ほど」 means 'the more... the more...'.
「〜わけがない」 means 'there is no way that...'.
「〜ので」 gives a reason in a polite or softer way than 'から'. (practice variation 24)
「〜まま」 means 'as is' or 'unchanged'. (practice variation 26)
「〜らしい」 can mean 'seems' or describe a typical characteristic.
「〜ことがある」 means 'sometimes' when talking about habitual actions. (practice variation 42)
「〜そうだ」 can indicate appearance, like 'it looks like'.
「〜ません」 is the polite negative form of verbs.
「〜です/〜ます」 is the standard polite ending for everyday conversation.
「〜に比べて」 means 'compared to...'.
「〜つもり」 expresses intent, like 'I plan to'.
「〜に違いない」 means 'must be' or 'no doubt'.
「〜ところ」 refers to a point in time like 'about to', 'in the middle of', or 'just finished'.
「〜ていく/〜てくる」 shows direction or change over time.
「〜なくてもいい」 means 'don't have to'.
「〜かもしれない」 means 'might be' or 'maybe'.
「〜うちに」 means 'while' or 'before' something changes.
「〜て以来」 means 'since doing'.
「〜なら」 means 'if' or 'in the case of'.
「〜せいで」 indicates a negative cause ('because of').
「〜に加えて」 means 'in addition to...'.
「〜たら」 is a conditional 'if/when'.
「〜たり〜たり」 lists representative actions.
「〜にする」 means 'decide on'.
「〜ながらも」 means 'even while' or 'though'.
「〜というより」 means 'rather than' or 'more like'.
「〜そうです」 can report hearsay ('I heard that...').
「〜ておく」 means 'do in advance' or 'leave as is'.
「〜あきらめないで」 expresses encouragement like 'don't give up'.
「〜ために」 means 'for the sake of' or indicates purpose.
「〜でしょう」 adds probability or softens statements.
「〜ながら」 means 'while' (simultaneous action).
「〜ば」 forms a general conditional meaning 'if'.
「〜たことがある」 expresses past experience.
「〜ほど」 can express degree.
「〜ようと思う」 expresses a decision made at the moment of speaking.
「〜なければならない」 means 'must' or 'have to'.
「〜につれて」 means 'as... changes, ... also changes'.
「〜次第だ」 means 'it all depends on / as soon as'.
「〜やすい」 attaches to verb stems to mean 'easy to do'.
「〜なければならない」 means 'must' or 'have to' do something.
〜おそれがある means there is a risk that...
〜にすぎない means no more than...
"〜たびに" means "every time...".
「〜てもらう」 means "receive a favor" from someone. (practice variation 16)
[〜おかげで] expresses gratitude for a cause, "thanks to". (practice variation 52)
"〜ようと思う" expresses a decision made at the moment of speaking. (practice variation 18)
"〜て以来" means "since (doing)".
"〜ことができる" expresses ability, like "can do".
"〜ところ" refers to a point in time, like "about to", "in the middle of", or "just finished".
"〜というより" means "rather than" or "more like".
"「〜そうだ」can indicate appearance, like 'it looks like'. (community contribution 48)"
「〜つもり」 expresses intent, like 'I plan to' do something. (practice variation 12)
「〜に気づく」 means 'to notice / realize...'.
"〜かもしれない" means "might be" or "maybe".
